title: Dreamy Creamy Algerian Fish Tagine
description: A cultured and creamy Algerian dish featuring papelon, perfect for a sick day, made pescatarian-friendly and cooked in a copper pot. This recipe is a twist on traditional tagine, with a rich and comforting flavor profile.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a copper pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin and coriander and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cod fillets and cook for 2 minutes on each side, until browned.
Add the papelon, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Use a pressure cooker to speed up the cooking process, if desired, and cook for 5 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs, if desired.
